response.justification: Sarah Smith is a seasoned full-stack and systems software developer with 14+ years of experience, but her background is almost entirely in web, mobile, and application-layer software. The Principal Performance Architect role demands deep expertise in SoC/IP modeling, GPU architecture, SIMD/MIMD design, and deep-learning workload optimization at the hardware level — none of which appear anywhere in her resume. Her AI exposure is limited to application-layer agentic tooling, and her closest low-level work (DLang async I/O, C++ audio synthesis) falls far short of chip-level performance engineering.
strengths: 14+ years of diverse systems experience spanning embedded, mobile, web, and infrastructure demonstrates strong adaptability
Some low-level performance engineering background via DLang high-throughput services (millions of HTTP req/s, async I/O, fibers) and C++ audio synthesis
Current AI-adjacent work at re:cinq on agentic infrastructure and MCP tooling shows awareness of the modern AI ecosystem
concerns: No experience with SoC or IP modeling, GPU architecture, SIMD/MIMD pipelines, or hardware-software co-design at the chip level — the role's core requirements
No background in deep neural network training or inference optimization; 'AI' experience is strictly at the application and tooling layer
Principal-level seniority in this domain requires graduate-level hardware/architecture grounding; candidate holds a BS in CS with a career trajectory entirely in software

key_factors.factor: No DNN workload or GPU optimization background
